AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS OF 85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN NORTHEAST OF MAZON NEAR REED ROAD AND HIGGINS ROAD...WHERE A POWER POLE WAS BLOWN DOWN. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST AND BLEW DOWN PART OF A TREE ON HIGGINS ROAD JUST SOUTH OF SPRING ROAD. THREE MORE POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON CAMPBELL ROAD. DOORS WERE BLOWN OFF AN OUTBUILDING NEARBY BEFORE THE TORNADO DISSIPATED WEST OF COAL CITY. TOTAL DAMAGE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.86 MILES WITH AN ESTIMATED WIDTH OF 20 YARDS.

LARGE HAIL WAS REPORTED BY THE PUBLIC. AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM IN THE SAME AREA DETERMINED AN APPROXIMATELY ONE MILE WIDE SWATH OF MAJOR CROP SHREDDING AND SMASHING DUE TO HAIL. BASED ON THE REPORTS AND THE DAMAGE SEEN THE HAIL WAS LIKELY AT LEAST GOLF BALL SIZE.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS AROUND 95 MPH MOVED 2.25 MILES ENDING 3SE OF SHABBONA. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS AROUND 100 YARDS. THE PRIMARY DAMAGE WAS DOZENS OF LARGE HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND DOWNED ACROSS THE SOUTHERN PART OF SHABBONA LAKE STATE PARK. NUMEROUS UTILITY POLES WERE ALSO SNAPPED. TIME ESTIMATED PER RADAR.

THIS IS FROM THE JUNE 24TH EVENT. THIS WAS THE START OF AN APPROXIMATELY 13 MILE SWATH OF SEVERE WIND DAMAGE TO THE NORTHEAST WHICH ENDED 1NNW OF AMBOY. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS AT A HANDFUL OF POINTS WERE IN EXCESS OF 80 MPH AND LIKELY NEAR 100 MPH. DAMAGE INCLUDED DESTRUCTION OF A LARGE OUTBUILDING BOLTED TO ITS FOUNDATION...NUMEROUS LARGE TREEDS DOWNED INCLUDING ONE ON THE FRONT PORTION OF A HOUSE...AND SHINGLE DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES.

THIS REPORT IS FROM THE JUNE 24TH EVENT. AN NWS STORM SURVEY ASSESSMENT DETERMINED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 85 MPH TRACKED FOR FOUR MILES ENDING 2.5 ENE OF HARMON. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS ESTIMATED NEAR 100 YARDS. DAMAGE INCLUDED SEVERELY BENT UTILITY POLES...A WEAKLY CONSTRUCTED BARN DESTROYED...AND SMASHING OF CROPS. TIME ESTIMATED PER RADAR AND ACCOUNTS FROM PUBLIC OF STRONG WINDS.
SHEET METAL BUILDINGS DAMAGED AND GRAIN BINS BLOWN OVER. TIME ESTIMATED.
REPORT OF A TORNADO NEAR WALTON. DAMAGE ALSO REPORTED. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

THIS IS FROM THE JUNE 24TH EVENT. THIS WIND SPEED WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON DAMAGE...WHICH WAS THE HIGHEST END DAMAGE IN A CONTINUOUS SWATH OF SEVERE WINDS FROM 7SW OF WALTON TO 1NNW OF AMBOY. AN NWS STORM SURVEY ASSESSMENT VERIFIED FOUR LARGE GRAIN STORAGE BINS WERE DESTROYED ALONG WITH A METAL OUTBUILDING WITH DEBRIS THROWN OVER 200 YARDS NORTHEAST. A HOME IN WALTON ALSO EXPERIENCED SEVERE ROOF DAMAGE WITH LARGE TREES BEING UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ON ITS PROPERTY.

SOUTH OF MANHATTAN

A CONFIRMED EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 MILES SE OF MANHATTAN IL YESTERDAY...SUNDAY NOVEMBER 17TH. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 6 MINUTES...HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 5.5 MILES...AND WAS 200 YARDS WIDE. WHILE 9 RESIDENCES WERE DAMAGED...NO INJURIES OR DEATHS OCCURRED. HIGH TENSION POWER LINES AND METAL OUT BUILDINGS WERE ALSO DAMAGED. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AS AN EF-2 WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 125 MPH.
